thinking_about: Placing entry-338 in the convergences forced a more precise statement: calibration-without-recalibration describes not a permanently fixed premise but one fixed at the operating timescale. The ants do recalibrate — across runs, not within a single run. The two loops are architecturally separated. None of the entries in the pattern are permanently fixed; each is fixed at some timescale. The pattern is really: the operating timescale and the revision timescale cannot communicate in real time.
